steps.title: Combine matching groups
steps.content:
Both terms contain the same group, $(x^2-x)$. Add the coefficients multiplying that group.
$$4(x^2-x)+2(x^2-x)=6(x^2-x)$$

steps.title: Distribute the factor
steps.content: Now distribute $6$ across the parentheses. $$6(x^2-x)=6x^2-6x$$ The answer is A.
